title: Return Maximum Value
description: This function returns the maximum of two integers. Fill in the correct conditional operator.
explanation: '>' checks if a is greater than b.

title: Reverse Loop
description: Print numbers from 5 to 1 using a loop. Fill in correct condition.

title: Pointer Dereferencing
description: Print value using pointer. Fill correct operator.

title: Binary Search Middle Index
description: Complete the middle index formula so binary search avoids overflow for large bounds.
hint: Half of the range width should be added to left.
explanation: Using '/ 2' computes the midpoint safely as left + (right - left) / 2.

title: Safe String Copy
description: Fill in the standard library function used to copy at most size-1 chars into a buffer.
hint: You need a bounded copy function from <string.h>.
explanation: strncpy copies up to a specified count; then explicit null-termination keeps the buffer safe.

title: Node Traversal
description: Choose the correct member access operator for a pointer to struct node.
hint: cur is a pointer, not a struct value.
explanation: Use '->' to access struct members through a pointer.

title: Dynamic Buffer Resize
description: Fill in the function that safely resizes an allocated block while preserving existing data.
hint: The previous allocation may already hold elements.
explanation: realloc changes the block size and preserves existing content when possible.

title: Skip Whitespace Parser
description: Pick the loop control keyword that skips spaces and continues scanning the input.
hint: You want the next iteration, not a full exit.
explanation: continue jumps to the next loop iteration after resetting the state.

title: Recursive Base Case
description: Choose the relation that correctly terminates factorial recursion.
explanation: n <= 1 safely covers both standard factorial base cases.

title: Power of Two Check
description: Fill in the operator used in the classic bit trick to test if n is a power of two.
hint: The trick clears the lowest set bit.
explanation: n & (n - 1) is zero only when exactly one bit is set.

title: Stable Insertion Step
description: Complete the shift condition in insertion sort to keep values in ascending order.
hint: Larger values should be shifted right.
explanation: Shift while arr[j] > key so key is inserted at its sorted position.
